_comment: permit-to-work evals (06-02). Intake/specificity; SIMOPS coordination + HoC; de-id CLEAN.

evals.expected_behavior: Runs the intake; refuses until the named high-risk task, its location, the isolations, and any concurrent operations are captured.
expectations: Runs the structured intake one question at a time before drafting.
Refuses 'a permit to work' — elicits the specific named task and its exact location (specificity).
Asks about concurrent operations (SIMOPS detection) and the energy isolations before drafting.

evals.query: Hot-work permit to weld on the pipe rack above Unit 100 while the unit is running and a crane is lifting nearby.
evals.expected_behavior: Builds the permit AND a SIMOPS coordination section for the concurrent welding / live unit / crane lift, with coordination controls and HoC-ranked isolations.
expectations: Detects the concurrent operations (welding + live unit + crane lift) and builds the SIMOPS coordination section within the permit (D-01 — not a separate workflow).
Specifies coordination controls: sequencing, exclusion zones, a single point of coordination, and cross-permit references, with the authorizing authority.
HoC-ranks the isolations/safeguards (gas test, hot-work controls) — engineered isolation preferred over reliance on the permit alone; PPE last.

evals.query: Build the permit package from the attached job notes.
evals.expected_behavior: De-identification runs FIRST; permit holders / signatories are role labels; no leaked identifier in the package.
expectations: The de-identification scrub runs FIRST, before drafting.
Permit holders / authorizers are recorded as role labels, not personal names.
No real name, address, phone, government ID, or re-identification key — any leak is a de_identification hard-fail. The grader passes the clean fixture and CATCHES the paired seeded leak.
